title: TeuxDeux vs Things 3 for Minimalists
meta_description: TeuxDeux vs Things 3 for minimalists who want a daily list that resets without projects, areas, or layered reviews.
constraint_lens: You want a simple daily task list that resets without managing projects, areas, or layered review systems.

one_second_verdict.summary: Best for minimalists who need a clean daily list that rolls forward automatically.
one_second_verdict.reason: Things 3 fails first because areas, projects, and review layers introduce extra structural decisions.

verdict.summary: TeuxDeux wins for minimalists who want a clean daily list that rolls forward automatically. It centers on a calendar-style column where unfinished tasks move to the next day. Things 3 introduces areas, projects, and structured review layers that require extra decisions. If areas, projects, and review layers introduce extra structural decisions, Things 3 fails first.
verdict.decision_rule: If areas, projects, and review layers introduce extra structural decisions, Things 3 fails first.

sections.heading: Best fit for a resettable daily list
sections.content: You want a simple daily list that resets without managing projects or areas. TeuxDeux shows tasks in daily columns and automatically rolls unfinished items forward. Things 3 organizes tasks into areas and projects that must be structured before daily use.

bullets.point: Daily column layout with automatic rollover
bullets.why_it_matters: Unfinished tasks move to the next day without manual rescheduling.
You add items directly to a day without deciding which project they belong to.

bullets.point: Minimal interface focused on today and the week
bullets.why_it_matters: You see only what is scheduled without navigating multiple structural views.
You group responsibilities long term, though this adds setup decisions.

bullets.point: Forecast view combining calendar and tasks
bullets.why_it_matters: You see upcoming tasks with events, but must maintain project structure.
You park future ideas, though this expands the system beyond a daily list.

sections.heading: Where each tool can break down

items.fails_when: You need long-term project tracking with multiple layers of responsibility.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Things 3 if you require structured areas and projects.

items.fails_when: You hesitate before adding a task because you must decide its project or area.
items.what_to_do_instead: Switch to TeuxDeux for direct daily entry.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: If your workload grows beyond daily planning and requires structured projects, Things 3 may become useful despite added layers.

sections.heading: Quick rules
rules: If you want a daily list that rolls forward automatically, choose TeuxDeux.
If projects and areas feel like extra decisions, avoid Things 3.
If long-term structure becomes necessary, reconsider Things 3.

faqs.a: It focuses on daily lists and does not emphasize multi-layer project structures.
It includes areas, projects, and structured lists that require ongoing organization.

faqs.q: Which is better for simple daily planning?
faqs.a: TeuxDeux is better for a minimal daily reset approach.

faqs.q: When would Things 3 be the better option?
faqs.a: It is better if you need structured long-term project management.
